mission.title: Install and Configure Emissary Ingress on Kubernetes
mission.description: Emissary-ingress is an open-source, Kubernetes-native API gateway built on the Envoy Proxy, designed to simplify the ingress problem for cloud-native applications. Installing Emissary-ingress allows developers to manage ingress traffic efficiently without needing deep Kubernetes expertise.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Emissary-ingress Helm repository to your local Helm configuration. This allows you to install the Emissary-ingress chart from the specified repository.
```bash
helm repo add emissary-ingress https://getambassador.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Emissary-ingress
steps.description:
Install the Emissary-ingress chart in a new namespace called 'emissary'. This command will deploy the API gateway with the specified version.
```bash
helm install emissary-ingress emissary-ingress/emissary-ingress --namespace emissary --create-namespace --version 8.12.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Emissary-ingress pods are running successfully. This command will list the pods in the 'emissary' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n emissary
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding
steps.description:
To access the Emissary-ingress service locally, set up port forwarding. This will allow you to access the service on your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/emissary-ingress -n emissary 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Emissary-ingress release from your cluster. This command will remove all resources associated with the release.
```bash
helm uninstall emissary-ingress --namespace emissary
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were created during the installation. This ensures that no remnants of the installation remain.
```bash
kubectl delete crd emissaryingresses.getambassador.io
kubectl delete crd mappings.getambassador.io
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'emissary' namespace to clean up any remaining resources. This will remove the namespace and all resources within it.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace emissary
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's a good practice to back up your current configuration. You can export your current resources with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get all -n emissary -o yaml > emissary-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your local Helm repository is up to date with the latest charts available.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Emissary-ingress
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Emissary-ingress release to the latest version. This command will apply the upgrade using the specified version.
```bash
helm upgrade emissary-ingress emissary-ingress/emissary-ingress --namespace emissary --version 8.12.2
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Emissary-ingress pods are running the new version successfully. This command will list the pods in the 'emissary' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n emissary
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see pods in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the specific pod to diagnose the issue:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n emissary
``` 
Common issues include misconfigurations in the deployment or insufficient resource limits.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Emissary-ingress service is not reachable, ensure that the service is correctly configured and that the port forwarding is set up:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n emissary
``` 
Check if the correct ports are exposed and that the service is running.

troubleshooting.title: Ingress rules not working
troubleshooting.description:
If your ingress rules are not functioning as expected, verify the configurations:
```bash
kubectl get mappings -n emissary
``` 
Ensure that the mappings are correctly defined and that they point to the right services.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Emissary-ingress will result in running pods in the 'emissary' namespace, with the service accessible through port forwarding. You should also be able to configure ingress rules for your applications seamlessly.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add emissary-ingress https://getambassador.io
helm repo update

kubectl edit deployment emissary-ingress -n emissary
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: 500m
#     memory: 512Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes version 1.24 or higher and have installed Helm and kubectl.
